codeReview.summary: The rerun/fix review still fails because the feature handoff does not point to a commit that implements the scoped auth-navigation repair. The attached commit only adds timer-page coverage, while the editor auth helper stabilization cited in the handoff predates this feature, so there is no auditable feature-local fix for the assigned regression.

issues.description: The handoff records commit `568e34d` as the fix, but `git show --stat 568e34d` changes only `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts`. It does not touch `apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts` or `apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts`, so the attached fix commit is unrelated to the editor auth-navigation regression.

The handoff claims the regression was already fixed by earlier commits (`7bd0687`, `a7f3363`, `4fcb0d3`, `8260520`), but those commits predate this feature and are not packaged as the scoped implementation unit requested for `tracking-time-entry-editor-auth-navigation-fix`. As reviewed, the fix feature reports a green rerun rather than a traceable repair commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-test-worker` procedure is still being bypassed for rerun-only flaky-fix work: this worker reported success after green reruns without proving that the handoff commit directly contains the stabilization change.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md` steps 10-11 require the handoff commit to directly contain the fix and forbid treating a rerun-only pass as completion for flaky-fix features. The transcript skeleton for worker `958fdf47-fd52-41e7-895a-b616f1796d55` shows test reruns plus git inspection before the handoff attached unrelated commit `568e34d`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared testing-lane documentation identifies the `frontend-entry-editor` lane but does not capture that this lane's auth setup currently depends on `apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts`, which is where the earlier auth-helper changes landed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/testing-lanes.md` lists the `frontend-entry-editor` lane, and `git log -- apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts` shows the recent auth-related commits (`7bd0687`, `a7f3363`, `4fcb0d3`, `8260520`) all targeted `apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts`, not the commit attached in this feature handoff.

summary: Fail: the rerun proved the suite is green, but this fix feature still does not provide a scoped, traceable implementation for the editor auth-navigation regression. The attached commit is unrelated to the editor lane, so the original scrutiny failure remains unresolved.
